title: Ensure permissions on all resources are given only to the  tenancy administrator group

input_row.title: Ensure permissions on all resources are given only to the  tenancy administrator group
input_row.description: There is a built-in OCI IAM policy enabling the Administrators group to perform any action within a tenancy. In the OCI IAM console, this policy reads: Allow group Administrators to manage all-resources in tenancy Administrators create more users, groups, and policies to provide appropriate access to other groups. Administrators should not allow any-other-group full access to the tenancy by writing a policy like this - Allow group any-other-group to manage all-resources in tenancy The access should be narrowed down to ensure the least-privileged principle is applied.
input_row.rationale: Permission to manage all resources in a tenancy should be limited to a small number of users in the Administrators group for break-glass situations and to set up users/groups/policies when a tenancy is created. No group other than Administrators in a tenancy should need access to all resources in a tenancy, as this violates the enforcement of the least privilege principle.
input_row.audit: From CLI: 1. Run OCI CLI command providing the root compartment OCID to get the list of groups having access to manage all resources in your tenancy. oci iam policy list --compartment-id | grep -i "to manage all-resources in tenancy" 2. Verify the results to ensure only the Administrators group has access to manage all resources in tenancy. "Allow group Administrators to manage all-resources in tenancy"
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to OCI console. 2. Go to Identity -> Policies, In the compartment dropdown, choose the root compartment. Open each policy to view the policy statements. 3. Remove any policy statement that allows any group other than Administrators or any service access to manage all resources in the tenancy. From CLI: The policies can also be updated via OCI CLI, SDK and API, with an example of the CLI commands below:  Delete a policy via the CLI: oci iam policy delete --policy-id  Update a policy via the CLI: oci iam policy update --policy-id --statements Note: You should generally not delete the policy that allows the Administrators group the ability to manage all resources in the tenancy.
input_row.justification: The control involves checking IAM policies to ensure that only the Administrators group has full access to manage all resources. This task can be automated using AWS APIs, specifically with the `boto3` library to list and evaluate IAM policies. The second AI opinion correctly identifies that this can be automated in AWS using `boto3` methods such as `list_policies()` and `get_policy_version()`, which aligns with AWS capabilities for managing IAM policies.
input_row.automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: IAM Policies - API/CLI Method: `boto3.client('iam').list_policies()`, `boto3.client('iam').get_policy_version()` - Properties to Check: Policy documents to ensure only the Administrators group has permissions to manage all resources. - Pass Criteria: Only policies that grant the Administrators group management access are found; no other groups have such permissions. - Fail Criteria: Any policy is found that grants another group full access to manage all resources.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** oci_iam_policy_admin_group_only_all_resources_management

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **OCI API/SDK/CLI Calls:**  
  - Use the OCI Python SDK (`oci.iam.IdentityClient`) or OCI CLI to list all IAM policies in the root compartment.
    - Python SDK: `list_policies(compartment_id=<root_compartment_ocid>)`
    - CLI: `oci iam policy list --compartment-id <root_compartment_ocid>`
  - For each policy, enumerate all policy statements.
- **Process:**
  - For each policy statement, identify any that grant permission to "manage all-resources in tenancy".
    - This will typically match: `Allow group <group_name> to manage all-resources in tenancy`.
  - Extract the `<group_name>` for every statement granting this level of access.
  - Validate that ONLY the "Administrators" group is present in such statements.
- **Pass Criteria:**
  - ALL policy statements granting "manage all-resources in tenancy" exclusively reference the "Administrators" group.
- **Fail Criteria:**
  - ANY group other than "Administrators" is granted "manage all-resources in tenancy" permissions in any policy statement.
- **Sample OCI CLI command:**  
  `oci iam policy list --compartment-id <root_compartment_ocid> | grep -i "to manage all-resources in tenancy"`
- **Remediation Reference:**  
  If any non-Administrators group is found, update the policy via OCI Console, OCI CLI, or SDK to remove such permissions.  
  - CLI to update: `oci iam policy update --policy-id <policy_id> --statements <updated_statements>`
  - CLI to delete: `oci iam policy delete --policy-id <policy_id>`
- **References/Support:**  
  - CIS OCI Foundations Benchmark v3.0.0 §1.2
  - [OCI IAM Policies documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Identity/Concepts/policies.htm)

**SOURCE:** CIS_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure_Foundations_Benchmark_v3_0_0
step2_review:
It appears there is a misunderstanding in the task description. The context provided is related to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), not AWS. Therefore, references to AWS services, boto3, and AWS CLI are not applicable. I will proceed with the review based on OCI context.

**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as "Automated" is correct. The use of OCI Python SDK and OCI CLI to list and evaluate IAM policies is an appropriate automated method to check compliance with the control.
- The OCI Python SDK and OCI CLI provide the necessary capabilities to automate the retrieval and analysis of IAM policies.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically accurate. The use of `oci.iam.IdentityClient` to list policies and the process to evaluate policy statements are correct.
- The CLI command provided is valid and correctly uses `grep` to filter relevant policy statements.
- The program name `oci_iam_policy_admin_group_only_all_resources_management` is descriptive and follows a logical naming convention, although it could be slightly more concise.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved for brevity and clarity. Consider `oci_iam_admin_group_exclusive_access_check`.
- Ensure that the automation script handles pagination when listing policies, as the number of policies might exceed a single page.
- Consider logging or reporting mechanisms to document which policies were checked and the results of each check for audit trails.
- Include error handling in the automation script to manage API call failures or unexpected responses.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured, and the use of OCI API capabilities is correctly applied. The automation approach is sound and aligns with OCI's security best practices.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name for clarity and ensure the script handles pagination and includes error handling and logging for comprehensive audit trails.
